chemistry.scope_summary: Intramolecular [2+2] photocycloaddition of endo-2,4-dibromocyclopentadiene-1,8-dione using continuous-flow photochemistry with UV-B lamps (280-315 nm, 36W) in MeOH/H2O with H2SO4. Achieved quantitative conversion in 30 minutes residence time, enabling decagram-scale synthesis.

optimization_table.key_findings: Entry 1 with 0.1 M concentration, 0.400 mL/min flow rate, 5 min residence time gave 44% conversion under 9W UV-B broadband irradiation

figures.caption: Figure 2   The dione  7  and its hydrate(s)  7a  and  7b | Scheme 2   Chapman and Tsanaktsidis et al. improved synthesis of dimethyl 1,4-cubanedicarboxylate ( 1 ) 16,18 | Scheme 3   Synthesis of the cycloadduct  6  via Tsanaktsidis's reported  procedure

Scheme 4   [2+2] Photocycloaddition (1), double ring contraction (2),  acidification (3), and esterification step (4) for synthesis of dimethyl  1,4-cubanedicarboxylate ( 1 )

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Long reaction time (24 hours) and requirement for high-power mercury lamp (450 W)
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Dramatic time reduction to 5 minutes residence time with much lower power UV-B lamp (9 W), enabling continuous synthesis
translation_logic.what_changed: Reactor design from batch to continuous-flow photoreactor, light source from 450 W mercury lamp to 9 W UV-B lamp, reaction time from 1440 min to 5 min
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Reaction concentration (0.1 M), solvent system (MeOH/H2SO4), photochemical mechanism
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Reduced thermal hazards due to lower power light source and continuous processing

figure_descriptions: Custom-built flow photoreactors with UV-B lamps and numbering-up strategy for scale-up
Laboratory flow chemistry setup with syringe pumps and collection apparatus
Mechanistic schemes showing reaction pathway and synthesis route

conflicts: Residence time reported as both 30 min (in scope summary) and 5 min (in optimization table) - used table value as higher fidelity
notes: Well-documented flow photochemistry study with clear batch-to-flow translation. Some inconsistency in residence time reporting between different sections. Scale-up strategy clearly described with multiple reactor configurations.
